source-discipline.reason: This is a retroactive reconstruction. Every metric and claim is traceable to a git primitive (commit 9a120e9 or c914840, the DR, the skill file, migration 017) or a live data/guidebook.db query at HEAD c914840 (PRAGMA user_version=17, gap_mining 0 rows, GAP-283 status=OPEN, 296 gaps with mining_addressability unset). No conversation/turn narrative is invented; the provenance banner explicitly flags the absence of a transcript and limits the record to git/DB-derived facts.

adherence-logging-and-attestation.reason: This attestation plus the [DOCTRINE: 61c7f95] token on the consolidation commit satisfy rule #11(a)/(b) for the new sessions/ artifact. doctrine_sha matches HEAD:governance/mission-and-epistemics.md.

canonical-workplan.reason: The record places the gap-driven-mining infrastructure correctly relative to Phase B (companion to citation-miner, closes GAP-283 structurally), preserves phase ordering (no Phase E content authored), and corrects the stale 2026-05-23 handoff which listed gap-driven mining as a blocked owner-decision item.

deviations.reason: RETROACTIVE consolidation. The session-consolidator protocol expects the session that performed the work to consolidate at its own close from conversation history. Here a later session (2026-05-28) reconstructs the unconsolidated 2026-05-26 work from git + DB primitives because LATEST was stale and no record existed. The reconstruction is faithful but provenance-flagged; it is not a substitute for the live close that did not happen.

bias_direction: Reconstructing a session I did not run, three days later, with no transcript. The risk is over-smoothing: presenting diff-derived facts with more apparent process certainty than a lived record would, or implying GAP-283 is fully resolved when the DB shows the gap row still OPEN and the protocol unexercised (0 gap_mining rows). Mitigated by stating status precisely (PROPOSED DR, structural-only closure, deferred triage/pilot) and by the provenance banner. [RE-ATTESTATION 2026-06-11 per PI rule #11a: originally attested by session_2026-05-26-gap-driven-mining-protocol-design at doctrine 61c7f95; re-checked and re-attested at 3da73bd by session_2026-06-11-stage-4.3-gate-closure after doctrine delta 3fb2882 (Stage-1.1 tier-hierarchy revision: SR/meta T3->T2, Tier 1 expanded to admit directly-relevant high-control non-OT primary research, Tier 3 redefined as lower-control primary, Tiers 4-6 walled off). Assessment: Activity log; no tier-dependent doctrinal claims. Compliance unchanged.]
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An outside reviewer would object that any retroactive session record risks laundering reconstruction-from-diffs into the continuity chain as if it were lived session memory, and that authoring records for other sessions could normalize backfilling. Counter: (a) the provenance banner makes the reconstruction explicit and dates it; (b) every claim cites a SHA, file, or DB query, so the record is independently verifiable; (c) leaving the continuity break unrepaired already caused concrete harm (this turn nearly proceeded on a 3-day-stale plan), so a flagged reconstruction is strictly better than a silent gap.
